evidence: advancements in artificial intelligence (AI) may pose serious risks for many of the traditional tools used to identify individuals, including voice recognition, facial recognition or screening questions
generative AI systems may also be used by malicious actors to create more sophisticated cyber attacks (i.e., more realistic phishing or other attacks)
advancements in AI could also lead to an increase in the frequency of identity fraud or cyber attacks, which could cause us to incur increasing costs

limitations: ai_investment_intensity not scorable: excerpts contain no discussion of AI capex, infrastructure, or investment commitments
ai_narrative_concreteness not scorable: excerpts contain no discussion of AI use cases, initiatives, or deployments by EOG — AI is referenced only as an external threat vector in risk factors

ai_risk_disclosure_depth scored 3: named risk categories are specific (identity fraud, phishing, voice/facial recognition spoofing, generative AI-enabled attacks) but mechanisms linking to revenue/operations impact are generic; no quantification or named governance body present in excerpts
